reasoning: The resolution criteria define the test as whether Gukesh D's official Elo rating on the specified dates is 1% higher than his rating on the Wikipedia page as of October 26, 2025. Per our Wikipedia check on 2025-10-22, Gukesh's rating for October is 2752 (with recent articles confirming he dropped out of the top 10 due to poor form). A 1% increase requires reaching an Elo of at least 2780. This demands a massive 28-point net gain. FIDE ratings are published on the 1st of each month. For the first two resolution dates (Nov 2 and Nov 25), exactly the same rating list (November) will be in effect. Gukesh's only known competitive classical chess event in late October is the European Chess Club Cup, consisting of 7 rounds, making a +28 point jump practically impossible in a single month at his level. We anchor closely to the base rate of 0.007 for such leaps. The final date (January 24, 2026) gives two more months of tournaments to potentially mount a comeback, so the likelihood increases slightly, but remains very low given the +28 point requirement.
